title: GWR: Signal Failure Causing Delays near Didcot Parkway
locationName: Oxfordshire | Great Western Main Line | Didcot
evidence: Great Western Railway reports signal failure between Didcot Parkway and Swindon affecting services between London Paddington and South Wales. 10–20 minute delays expected. Network Rail engineers on site. No connection to weather conditions.

assessment.justification: Routine signal failure unrelated to the developing weather event. Reported via Railway Marketplace automated feed. Dispersed location with no relevance to the primary area of interest.
assessment.synthesis: Routine signal failure on the Great Western Main Line near Didcot. No connection to the developing meteorological event. Baseline noise only.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Routine pre-event signal failure. No contextual relevance.

title: GWR Cardiff–Bristol: Delays Due to Spray and Reduced Speeds
locationName: South Gloucestershire | Severn Tunnel Junction | Vale of Glamorgan
evidence: Great Western Railway advises 10–15 minute delays on Cardiff Central to Bristol Temple Meads services due to mandatory speed restrictions through the Severn Tunnel area. Heavy rain and track spray forcing reduced running speeds. Services operating but running behind schedule.

assessment.justification: GWR service alert confirmed. Speed restrictions through the Severn Tunnel area are consistent with onset of heavy rainfall in the region. First railway signal from the key Cardiff–Bristol corridor.
assessment.synthesis: Early delays on the Cardiff–Bristol rail corridor. First railway impact consistent with the emerging pattern of disruption across South Wales and the Severn estuary crossing.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Minor delay advisory. Early-stage signal.

title: TfW: Valley Lines Services Delayed by Surface Water
locationName: Rhondda Cynon Taf | Valley Lines | Rhondda
evidence: Transport for Wales reports speed restrictions on Valley Lines services across the Rhondda and Cynon valleys due to surface water on tracks. All services operating but with 15–25 minute delays. Train crew advised of conditions at key trackside sections.

assessment.justification: TfW service advisory consistent with early morning rainfall impacts in the South Wales valleys. Multiple valley lines affected simultaneously suggests a developing pattern rather than an isolated incident.
assessment.synthesis: Valley Lines services running late due to surface water on tracks across the Rhondda and Cynon valleys. Early morning indication of railway network vulnerability as conditions develop.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Advisory only. Pattern emerging across multiple valley lines.

title: TfW: All Services Suspended through Newport Station – Trackside Flooding
locationName: Newport | South Wales Main Line | Newport
evidence: Transport for Wales suspends all services through Newport station due to trackside flooding adjacent to the M4 underpass section. Flood water has breached trackside drainage. Network Rail engineers on site conducting safety assessment. Replacement bus services being arranged. No reopening timeline given.

assessment.justification: TfW service suspension confirmed. Trackside flooding near Newport correlates with simultaneous road flooding at M4 J24–J25. Dual road and rail disruption at Newport significantly degrades transport connectivity between England and South Wales.
assessment.synthesis: All rail services through Newport suspended due to trackside flooding. Combined with the M4 road closure, Newport is now a major bottleneck severing the England–South Wales transport corridor. Replacement buses face the same road conditions affecting all other traffic.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Service suspension confirmed. Dual road-rail impact at Newport corroborated.

title: TfW: Cardiff Central Severely Disrupted – London Services Cancelled
locationName: Cardiff | South Wales Main Line | Cardiff Central
evidence: Transport for Wales advises severe disruption at Cardiff Central station. All London Paddington services via GWR cancelled or heavily delayed. Newport station closure preventing normal through-working. Emergency timetable in place with terminations at Cardiff. Station concourse crowded with stranded passengers.

assessment.justification: TfW advisory confirmed. Cardiff Central disruption is a direct downstream consequence of the Newport suspension. London services are the busiest and most frequent on this corridor. Significant passenger impact is accumulating at Cardiff.
assessment.synthesis: Cardiff Central is severely disrupted with London services cancelled. The cascading effect of the Newport closure is now being felt across the entire South Wales main line. Passengers are unable to travel between Cardiff and London by either rail or road.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Confirmed disruption. Cascading impact from Newport rail suspension well-established.

title: TfW: Marches Line Speed Restrictions through Abergavenny
locationName: Monmouthshire | Marches Line | Brecon and Radnorshire
evidence: Transport for Wales imposes emergency 20mph speed restrictions on the Marches Line through Abergavenny due to trackside flooding adjacent to the River Usk. Significant delays accumulating on Crewe–Cardiff services. All trains running but severely delayed.

assessment.justification: TfW speed restriction confirmed. River Usk flooding proximate to the Marches Line near Abergavenny is a credible and expected impact. The Marches Line is the key north-south rail corridor through Southeast Wales and Herefordshire.
assessment.synthesis: Emergency speed restrictions on the Marches Line through Abergavenny due to River Usk flooding. Further delays accumulating on north-south services, extending railway disruption through the primary event corridor.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Speed restriction confirmed. Consistent with River Usk flood conditions.

title: TfW: Hereford Station – Cascading Delays on Marches Line
evidence: Transport for Wales reports all Marches Line services through Hereford running 30–45 minutes late as a consequence of speed restrictions imposed south of Abergavenny. Platform capacity being managed. Alternative travel advice issued via social media and station screens.

assessment.justification: TfW advisory confirms cascade delays reaching Hereford. These are a direct consequence of the Abergavenny restrictions and Newport suspension. Demonstrates the geographic spread of railway impact into Herefordshire.
assessment.synthesis: Marches Line delays cascading through to Hereford. Railway disruption now extends from Newport through Abergavenny to Hereford, affecting the full length of the primary impact corridor by rail.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Cascade delays confirmed at Hereford. Pattern consistent with upstream restrictions.

title: GWR: Speed Restrictions near Colwall Tunnel, Great Malvern
evidence: Great Western Railway advises speed restrictions near Colwall Tunnel west of Great Malvern due to waterlogged trackbed. Services between Hereford and Worcester running 20–30 minutes late. Network Rail engineers monitoring the situation.

assessment.justification: GWR advisory for Colwall Tunnel area. Waterlogged trackbed consistent with rainfall in the Malvern Hills area. Peripheral to the core event but reflects the eastern extent of railway disruption.
assessment.synthesis: Speed restrictions near Colwall Tunnel extend the railway impact picture into Worcestershire. Peripheral to the core event but consistent with the eastern spread of disruption beyond the Welsh Marches.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Speed restriction advisory. Peripheral location.
